name: seren-memory
description: Gives GitHub Copilot and the user access to the SerenMemory store - search, write, brief, consolidate, and manage drafts.

serenMemory.endpoint.description: Base URL of the SerenMemory service. Default port 7420 matches SerenMemory's own default.

serenMemory.startCommand.description: Shell command used to start the SerenMemory service when it is not reachable. Application-scoped so workspace settings cannot override it (security: prevents a malicious .vscode/settings.json from injecting a different command).

serenMemory.suppressStartPrompt.description: Suppress the 'service not reachable - start it?' prompt on extension activation. Use this if you intentionally run SerenMemory only for some projects.

serenMemory.bearerTokenHint.markdownDescription:
**Bearer token** is stored securely in the OS keychain, not here.

To set or update it, run the command:

`Seren Memory: Set Bearer Token` (via `Ctrl+Shift+P`)

languageModelTools.name: seren_memory_search
languageModelTools.userDescription: Search SerenMemory for relevant past context.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Search the SerenMemory store for relevant facts, preferences, or context. The main retrieval path - call this BEFORE answering anything that might benefit from past context about the user or project. Returns ranked hits across the requested tiers; short-term is weighted highest (most recent context), long-term gets an evidence-count multiplier so well-established facts outrank one-offs.

n_results.description: Max hits to return after merging and ranking across tiers.

include_superseded.description: Include superseded long-term entries (historical 'what the user USED to think').

languageModelTools.name: seren_memory_write
languageModelTools.userDescription: Write a memory to the short- or near-term tier.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Store a memory entry in the short-term or near-term tier. Use short-term ('short') for transient facts (session notes, observations); use near-term ('near') for future-tense intents ('bring this up later', 'do X next time'). LONG-TERM CANNOT BE WRITTEN DIRECTLY - long-term is the gated tier (writes happen only through consolidation). To get a fact into long-term immediately, use seren_memory_promote_now after writing to short.

content.description: The memory text. For tier='near' this is the intent statement.

topic.description: Topic label - drives consolidation clustering. Use the same topic for related entries so the consolidator can cluster them.

tier.description: 'short' for working memory, 'near' for open loops / future intents.

languageModelTools.name: seren_memory_brief
languageModelTools.userDescription: Submit a steering brief for the next consolidation.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Submit a steering brief for the next consolidator cycle. The brief tells the consolidator what mattered in this period and which topics to promote vs. ignore. promote_hints and noise_hints are matched against entry topics+content during cluster promotion - they ARE the lever for steering what becomes long-term memory. Use them.

summary.description: Free-text summary of what mattered in this period.

promote_hints.description: Topic phrases worth remembering durably. Lowers cluster-promotion threshold for matching entries.

noise_hints.description: Topic phrases that are noise. Raises cluster-promotion threshold for matching entries.

completed_intents.description: Near-term intents that look done; the consolidator promotes them to long-term as a record.

languageModelTools.name: seren_memory_consolidate
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Trigger the SerenMemory consolidator to cluster and synthesise short-term memories into long-term drafts. REQUIRES a configured consolidator model (a local LLM, typically Nemotron-3-Nano or similar). Without one, the synthesis falls back to mechanical (longest entry per cluster). For deploys with no local LLM, prefer waiting for the next scheduled cycle.

languageModelTools.name: seren_memory_preserve_verbatim
languageModelTools.userDescription: Flag a short-term entry for verbatim promotion.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Mark a short-term entry for verbatim promotion on the next consolidator cycle - preserves exact phrasing instead of having the consolidator synthesise. Use when the WORDS matter, not just the gist (a specific quote, a precise spec, a verbatim user statement). Also pins the entry so it survives aging until the cycle runs.

short_id.description: ID of the short-term entry to flag verbatim.

languageModelTools.name: seren_memory_promote_now
languageModelTools.userDescription: Promote a short-term entry to long-term immediately.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Immediately move a short-term entry to long-term verbatim, skipping the consolidator cycle entirely. Use when you KNOW a fact is durable and shouldn't wait for the next cycle - for example, after the user explicitly tells you to remember something. This is the only way to put a specific fact into long-term immediately (direct long-term writes are forbidden by design - consolidator owns long-term writes).

short_id.description: ID of the short-term entry to promote immediately.

languageModelTools.name: seren_memory_forget_long
languageModelTools.userDescription: Flag a long-term memory to be forgotten (the Lacuna gate).
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Flag a long-term memory for the consolidator to handle. PII keywords (ssn, password, credit card, etc.) trigger purge; other reasons demote the entry (zero evidence_count, marks as demoted). Never a surgical delete - the Lacuna gate is a FLAG, not a scalpel. Use when the user asks to forget something, or when you discover a long-term fact is wrong or outdated.

long_id.description: ID of the long-term entry to flag.

reason.description: Why this should be forgotten. Specific reasons help the consolidator decide (purge vs. demote vs. supersede).

languageModelTools.name: seren_memory_complete_intent
languageModelTools.userDescription: Mark a near-term intent as acted on.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Mark a near-term intent as ACTED ON (not merely referenced). The consolidator promotes completed intents to long-term as a record of 'we did this'. Without this call, intents accumulate forever and never promote.

intent_id.description: ID of the near-term entry to mark complete.

languageModelTools.name: seren_memory_list_drafts
languageModelTools.modelDescription: List consolidation drafts awaiting model review. Filter by status: pending, approved, rejected, or requires_selection.

status.description: Filter drafts by status. Omit for all drafts.

languageModelTools.name: seren_memory_draft_chain
languageModelTools.userDescription: Show every synthesis attempt for one cluster.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Retrieve the full redraft chain for a draft - all attempts, their critiques, and statuses. Use this when a draft has requires_selection status to compare all attempts before selecting the best one.

draft_id.description: ID of any draft in the chain.

languageModelTools.name: seren_memory_approve_draft
languageModelTools.userDescription: Approve a draft into long-term memory.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Approve a pending consolidation draft, committing it to long-term memory.

draft_id.description: ID of the draft to approve.

note.description: Optional confirmation note recorded with the approval.

languageModelTools.name: seren_memory_reject_draft
languageModelTools.userDescription: Reject a draft with a critique to trigger a redraft.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: Reject a consolidation draft with a critique. The consolidator will redraft up to the configured retry limit. Once the limit is reached, the chain moves to requires_selection. CRITIQUE SHOULD BE SPECIFIC - 'you conflated X with Y, separate them and emphasise Y' beats 'wrong vibe'. The next attempt sees the critique + all previous attempts as steering.

draft_id.description: ID of the draft to reject.

critique.description: Specific reason for rejection; guides the redraft. Be concrete about what's wrong.

languageModelTools.name: seren_memory_select_draft
languageModelTools.userDescription: Select the best draft from a requires_selection chain.
languageModelTools.modelDescription: When a draft chain has requires_selection status, select the best draft from all attempts to commit to long-term memory. Use seren_memory_draft_chain first to compare all attempts. Optionally provide edited_content to commit a revised version instead of the draft as-is - use this when none of the chain attempts is quite right but one is closest, and a small edit fixes it.

draft_id.description: ID of the draft attempt to select as the best.

edited_content.description: Optional revised text to commit instead of the draft. Omit to commit the draft unchanged. Empty string is rejected with an error - use this field only when you genuinely have a revision.

note.description: Optional note recorded with the selection.
